transcript: Okay, no, no, let's just start with middle C. Middle C is right here. Like this. That's right! Well done! Professor Yang, how do you play the middle C on the violin? Let me show you. Now, all you have to do is put three fingers on this string here, and... Whoa! Can I try? Yes, you can. Be very careful. Okay. Third finger on the string... Right to here. Okay, 3rd finger on the string. Is it here? Yep, close enough. Look, I just learned how to play twinkle, Twinkle Little Stars in two months. That's awesome! I also learned how to play Twinkle, Twinkle sure you're not too light or too heavy. It's a great combination between weight, contact, and speed. Like this? Yeah, that's it. The sound ain't bad. Whoa, it's too light, it's too... No, it's too heavy, too much weight. But I literally didn't change anything. Well, sorry to say, the bow is actually heavy at the frog and light at the tip. So you literally have to recalibrate the pressure for every single millimeter of the bow. I just did seven hours of piano practice today. How productive. How much did you practice? I also practiced seven hours of violin today. Oh, my neck. I think I need a massage. You need to see a physio. A, B. Remember the space. Okay. A, B. Say what! Remember the space. Okay. A, B. Say whaaaat! Wow. I can finally play all the notes on the fingerboard now. Well, kinda. Good, now we can move on to double stops. Time to practice your thirds, your sixths, your octaves, and your tenths. And don't forget fingered octaves. Now let's spend the next five years trying to play double stops. Okay, great! Did you practice your chords? What is a chord? Well, it's three to four notes played at the same time. It's literally impossible to touch three strings at the same time. Thank you. Well, it's three to four notes played at the same time. It's literally impossible......to touch three strings at the same time. Easy, I didn't think so. Okay. Ha, ha, ha, ha, way, did I tell you that you need a new violin? Because no way you can win a competition with that cardboard box. Well, um, how much does a good violin cost? Well, anywhere from $200,000 to $10,000,000.
